# ISF fixtures

Synthetic Microsoft Ink Serialized Format `.isf` files for
`tests/test_isf.py`, generated by `make_fixture.py` in this directory:

```sh
cd core
uv run python tests/fixtures/isf/make_fixture.py
```

- `pen-pressure-tilt.isf` — ink-space rect (0,0,20000,15000 HIMETRIC),
  drawing-attributes block (blue, 150 himetric = 1.5 mm pen width),
  stroke descriptor (NORMAL_PRESSURE, AZIMUTH, ALTITUDE, TIMER_TICK),
  metric block overriding pressure to 0..4096, two strokes:
  - stroke 1: 8-point sine wave, pressure ramp 400..3200, azimuth 90.0
    deg, altitude 45.0 deg, 10 ms timer steps;
  - stroke 2: 5-point horizontal line, constant pressure 2048, azimuth
    0 deg, altitude 90 deg.
  Packet compression coverage: indexed Huffman table 2 (X/Y/pressure/
  timer), plain bit-packing (azimuth), delta-delta bit-packing
  (altitude).
- `highlighter.isf` — drawing attributes with COLORREF yellow,
  transparency byte 100, rectangle pen tip and raster op 9 (MaskPen =
  highlighter); one 6-point stroke; covers the uncompressed packet path
  (algo byte 0x00 = big-endian int32) and plain bit-packing.
- `xy-only.isf` — the spec's "simple example": no tables at all, one
  Huffman-compressed X/Y stroke rendered with default drawing
  attributes.

## Provenance

These are NOT files from a real device or app and contain no third-party
ink data. `make_fixture.py` is a from-scratch ISF encoder written
against the MS-ISF specification (published under the Microsoft Open
Specification Promise) and Microsoft's MIT-licensed WPF ISF codec
(github.com/dotnet/wpf). The primitives it shares with the reader are
pinned byte-for-byte in `tests/test_isf.py` by hand-computed spec
values, and the reader is additionally validated against real
Microsoft-encoded ISF from the MIT-licensed dotnet/wpf-test repository
(corpus-gated; see `corpus/third-party/MANIFEST.toml`).

## Attribution

Format knowledge derives from the MS-ISF specification (Microsoft Open
Specification Promise) and dotnet/wpf (MIT, .NET Foundation and
Contributors). The fixture files themselves are CC0-1.0.
